name: Legal Services of Northern Michigan, INC.
description_short: Nonprofit law firm providing free legal representation to low-income residents across Northern Michigan for civil matters including housing, family law, and benefits cases.
description_long:
Legal Services of Northern Michigan (LSNM) has operated since 1966 as a nonprofit law firm dedicated to serving low-income and marginalized communities across 36 counties in Northern Lower Michigan and the Upper Peninsula. The organization addresses gaps in the civil justice system by providing free legal advice and representation to eligible clients who cannot afford private attorneys. With 15 experienced lawyers and support staff distributed across 6 regional offices, LSNM maintains a mission centered on access to justice for vulnerable populations.

LSNM offers free legal services across a broad range of civil matters including consumer cases, family law, housing problems (evictions and foreclosures), domestic violence and sexual assault representation, elder abuse cases, and government benefits access. Their practice areas explicitly serve clients facing basic needs insecurity and personal safety threats. The organization does not charge attorney fees to any clients and explicitly avoids criminal matters, focusing exclusively on civil legal issues.

LSNM is distinguished by its 50+ year operational history, community-based governance structure that includes formerly incarcerated clients on the Board of Directors, and explicit priority-setting based on client vulnerability and poverty impact. The organization is led by Executive Director Kenneth Penokie with two Deputy Directors. Their funding model relies on federal, state, and local government grants plus private foundation and individual donations, ensuring they remain debt-free and mission-aligned without profit incentives.

While LSNM provides genuinely free legal help to eligible low-income clients, the organization explicitly acknowledges limited resources and must prioritize cases based on funder requirements, board direction, and client community needs. Prospective clients should verify eligibility and understand that case acceptance depends on organizational priorities and available capacity at their local office.
pros: Completely free legal services with zero attorney fees for eligible clients
50+ years of continuous operation serving Northern Michigan communities since 1966
15 experienced staff lawyers with presence in 6 offices across 36 counties
Handles multiple priority areas including housing, domestic violence, sexual assault, elder abuse, and government benefits
Board governance includes attorneys, formerly incarcerated clients, and partner agency representatives
Federal, state, and local government funding ensures mission alignment without profit incentives
Explicit focus on serving vulnerable populations facing poverty and discrimination
cons: Limited resources mean not all cases can be accepted; case priority determined by organizational criteria
Geographic service limited to Northern Lower Michigan and Upper Peninsula only
Does not handle criminal matters, redirecting clients to court-appointed counsel for criminal cases
Eligibility requirements restrict services to low-income and marginalized populations
Small staff of 15 lawyers across 36 counties limits availability and response capacity
best_for: Low-income residents facing eviction, foreclosure, or housing discrimination in Northern Michigan
Domestic violence or sexual assault survivors seeking legal protection and representation
Individuals attempting to access government benefits (income, healthcare, food security)
Elderly residents experiencing abuse seeking legal intervention and protection
diagnosis: LSNM is ideal for low-income residents across Northern Michigan facing civil legal crises in housing, family matters, domestic violence, or government benefits—but only if eligible and if their case aligns with organizational priorities. The main caveat is that while services are genuinely free, limited capacity means not all eligible clients can be served, and case acceptance depends on LSNM's current resource allocation and stated priorities.
services: Free legal advice and representation for civil cases
Housing problem representation (eviction defense and foreclosure cases)
Domestic violence legal services and protection orders
Government benefits access assistance (income, healthcare, food security)
Local office consultations across 6 regional offices
Case priority determination based on vulnerability and organizational capacity
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
meta_description: Nonprofit law firm providing free legal representation to Northern Michigan residents for housing, family law, and benefits.
